headline: Political financing transparency law remains absent since 2018 promise
context: Despite a 2018 election promise, Malaysia still lacks political financing legislation as of 2025, leaving party funding opaque.

cards.big: Political financing law promised in 2018 still absent 7 years later
cards.sub: Party funding sources remain undisclosed with no legal requirement for transparency

cards.big: Societies Act 1966 requires minimal financial reporting by political parties
cards.sub: No statutory obligation to disclose donors, amounts, or funding sources

cards.big: Transparency International CPI linked opaque political funding to corruption risk
cards.sub: Right to information about how political power is financed is a governance right

cards.big: Estimated RM2.4 billion spent in GE15 with zero public disclosure of sources
cards.sub: Voters cannot assess whether elected representatives serve donors or constituents

cards.big: Without knowing who funds politicians, voters cannot know who governs them
Institut Darul Ehsan drafted model political financing bill in 2023
